content:
Path A “Show me my gap in 2 minutes” (recommended)
Take the interactive scorecard:
https://dharmik2510.github.io/agent-harness-blueprint/diagnose
You’ll see which pillar is starving, Instructions, State, Verification, Scope, or Lifecycle — and what to fix first.
Or from terminal:
git clone https://github.com/Dharmik2510/agent-harness-blueprint
cd agent-harness-blueprint && npm install
npm run score -- /path/to/your/repoPath B “Fix my repo today” (15 minutes)

• Open your project in VS Code with Copilot Chat

• Run /init to draft .github/copilot-instructions.md

• Copy the template packs from the repo (templates/universal/ + templates/copilot/minimal/)

• Run bash scripts/init.sh before every agent session

• End every session by updating PROGRESS.md
Step-by-step guide: https://dharmik2510.github.io/agent-harness-blueprint/start-here/quick-start
Path C “Convince me with evidence” (45 minutes)
Do Lab 01, Baseline vs harness: run the same feature task twice on a sample React app — once with prompts only, once with a minimal harness.
Lab: https://dharmik2510.github.io/agent-harness-blueprint/labs/lab-01-baseline-vs-harness
Most people feel the difference in one session.
The four-stage journey (how the course is organized)
Diagnose ──► Learn ──► Build ──► Prove
│ │ │ │
Scorecard 9 modules 6 labs Re-score
(2 min) (~8 min ea) (hands-on) (number ↑)Press enter or click to view image in full size

A Copilot session that actually works
